sourceMovie.title: Dragon Ball: The Path to Power
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dragon Ball: The Path to Power works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: My Hero Academia: Heroes Rising
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragon Ball: The Path to Power clicked for you, My Hero Academia: Heroes Rising (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dragon Ball: The Path to Power?
faq.answer: Try My Hero Academia: Heroes Rising, That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime the Movie: Scarlet Bond, Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna—each shares DNA with Dragon Ball: The Path to Power while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dragon Ball: The Path to Power?
faq.answer: Dragon Ball: The Path to Power (1996) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dragon Ball: The Path to Power part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Dragon Ball Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
